captions.text: In this course, you've learned how to engineer memory systems
that transform stateless LLMs into persistent Memory-Aware Agents.
You started by understanding why agents fail without memory,
then built a Memory Manager with persistent stores
for different memory types.
You scaled tool use with semantic tool memory,
built extractions and consolidation pipelines
that turn raw conversations into durable knowledge,
and wired up self-updating agent loops
that let your agents refine what it knows autonomously.
Finally, you brought it all together
into a fully stateful Memory Aware Agent,
one that loads prior context at startup,
checkpoints its reasoning and persists across sessions.
The patterns you've learned here,
memory modeling, semantic retrieval,
extraction, consolidation, and write back,
are the building blocks for production agents
that actually improve over time.
What you've built in this course is a foundation
for Memory Engineering.
Take these patterns, adapt them to your own use case
and build agents that don't just respond,
they remember and get better.
I am looking forward to seeing
what you will build on your own.
